key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide layer than detonation-sprayed T-400, giving better hot-corrosion resistance in molten solar salt at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal storage containers, HVAF coating appears to be the more reliable choice for extending service life under prolonged nitrate-salt exposure.
method: T-400 coatings were deposited on ASTM A516 steel by HVAF and detonation spraying.
Samples were exposed to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h.
Phase, SEM, and elemental mapping were used to compare oxide formation, salt ingress, and degradation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF maintained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O enriched surface with minimal salt penetration, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation.
watch_item: The study is based on a specific salt composition and temperature, so performance may differ under thermal cycling or other CSP operating conditions.
paper_title: Hot Corrosion Behavior of Thermal Sprayed Using HVAF and DS Techniques with Tribaloy-400 Coatings for Solar Energy Storage Container
